TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @misc{Wiki_szy_banic, title = banic, year = 2025, month = May, day = 5, note = {In Wikipedia [Sakizaya]}, url = {https://szy.wikipedia.org/wiki/banic} }

S.FORM: == banic == ŤÖĻÁÄČ siwkay nu balulu u Pangcah a kamu sa , u mapiyas saan. adada' ku bili, besuc saan ku tayi', tanunanum saan ku tayi'. tinaku a kamu mabanic ŤÖĻÁÄČ pi:na tu a demiad mabanic kaku. mabanicay ŤÖĻÁÄČÁöĄ tadaaheteng ku nu mabanicay a tayi'. mibanic śúÉŤÖĻÁÄČ mibanic ku cucu nu katalalan. mibanicay śúÉŤÖĻÁÄČÁöĄ na u canan ku mibanicay i tisuwan? namabanic ŤÖĻÁÄČťĀéšļÜ namabanic kaku i nacilal namukan tu cucu nu katalalan. sakibanic ś≠ĘÁÄČŤó• sakibanic ku mangazuway a papah nu kayabes. u sulit nu Hulam atu Amilika Diarrhea, also spelled diarrhoea, is the condition of having at least three loose or liquid bowel movements each day. It often lasts for a few days and can result in dehydration due to fluid loss. Signs of dehydration often begin with loss of the normal stretchiness of the skin and irritable behaviour. This can progress to decreased urination, loss of skin color, a fast heart rate, and a decrease in responsiveness as it becomes more severe.Loose but non-watery stools in babies who are breastfed, however, may be normal.
S.PHON: == bani ¶ == ** siwkaj nu bar~…ĺur~…ĺu u paŇč ¶ańß a kamu sa , u mapijas saan. adada Ē~ ° ku bir~…ĺi, b…ôsu ¶ saan ku taji Ē~ °, tanunanum saan ku taji Ē~ °. tinaku a kamu mabani ¶ ** pi:na tu a d…ômiad mabani ¶ kaku. mabani ¶aj *** tadaańß…ôt…ôŇč ku nu mabani ¶aj a taji Ē~ °. mibani ¶ *** mibani ¶ ku ¶u ¶u nu katar~…ĺar~…ĺan. mibani ¶aj **** na u ¶anan ku mibani ¶aj i tisuwan? namabani ¶ **** namabani ¶ kaku i na ¶ir~…ĺar~…ĺ namukan tu ¶u ¶u nu katar~…ĺar~…ĺan. sakibani ¶ *** sakibani ¶ ku maŇčazuwaj a papańß nu kajab…ôs. u sur~…ĺit nu ńßur~…ĺam atu amir~…ĺika diarrńß…ôa, ar~…ĺso sp…ôr~…ĺr~…ĺ…ôd diarrńßo…ôa, is tńß…ô ¶ondition of ńßa*iŇč at r~…ĺ…ôast tńßr…ô…ô r~…ĺoos…ô or r~…ĺi*uid bow…ôr~…ĺ mo*…ôm…ônts …ôa ¶ńß daj. it oft…ôn r~…ĺasts for a f…ôw dajs and ¶an r…ôsur~…ĺt in d…ôńßjdration du…ô to fr~…ĺuid r~…ĺoss. signs of d…ôńßjdration oft…ôn b…ôgin witńß r~…ĺoss of tńß…ô normar~…ĺ str…ôt ¶ńßin…ôss of tńß…ô skin and irritabr~…ĺ…ô b…ôńßa*iour. tńßis ¶an progr…ôss to d…ô ¶r…ôas…ôd urination, r~…ĺoss of skin ¶or~…ĺor, a fast ńß…ôart rat…ô, and a d…ô ¶r…ôas…ô in r…ôsponsi*…ôn…ôss as it b…ô ¶om…ôs mor…ô s…ô*…ôr…ô.r~…ĺoos…ô but non-wat…ôrj stoor~…ĺs in babi…ôs wńßo ar…ô br…ôastf…ôd, ńßow…ô*…ôr, maj b…ô normar~…ĺ.
S.FORM: == banic == ŤÖĻÁÄČ siwkay nu balulu u Pangcah a kamu sa , u mapiyas saan. adada' ku bili, besuc saan ku tayi', tanunanum saan ku tayi'. tinaku a kamu mabanic ŤÖĻÁÄČ pi:na tu a demiad mabanic kaku. mabanicay ŤÖĻÁÄČÁöĄ tadaaheteng ku nu mabanicay a tayi'. mibanic śúÉŤÖĻÁÄČ mibanic ku cucu nu katalalan. mibanicay śúÉŤÖĻÁÄČÁöĄ na u canan ku mibanicay i tisuwan? namabanic ŤÖĻÁÄČťĀéšļÜ namabanic kaku i nacilal namukan tu cucu nu katalalan. sakibanic ś≠ĘÁÄČŤó• sakibanic ku mangazuway a papah nu kayabes. u sulit nu Hulam atu Amilika Diarrhea, also spelled diarrhoea, is the condition of having at least three loose or liquid bowel movements each day. It often lasts for a few days and can result in dehydration due to fluid loss. Signs of dehydration often begin with loss of the normal stretchiness of the skin and irritable behaviour. This can progress to decreased urination, loss of skin color, a fast heart rate, and a decrease in responsiveness as it becomes more severe.Loose but non-watery stools in babies who are breastfed, however, may be normal.
S.PHON: == bani ¶ == ** siwkaj nu bar~…ĺur~…ĺu u paŇč ¶ańß a kamu sa , u mapijas saan. adada Ē~ ° ku bir~…ĺi, b…ôsu ¶ saan ku taji Ē~ °, tanunanum saan ku taji Ē~ °. tinaku a kamu mabani ¶ ** pi:na tu a d…ômiad mabani ¶ kaku. mabani ¶aj *** tadaańß…ôt…ôŇč ku nu mabani ¶aj a taji Ē~ °. mibani ¶ *** mibani ¶ ku ¶u ¶u nu katar~…ĺar~…ĺan. mibani ¶aj **** na u ¶anan ku mibani ¶aj i tisuwan? namabani ¶ **** namabani ¶ kaku i na ¶ir~…ĺar~…ĺ namukan tu ¶u ¶u nu katar~…ĺar~…ĺan. sakibani ¶ *** sakibani ¶ ku maŇčazuwaj a papańß nu kajab…ôs. u sur~…ĺit nu ńßur~…ĺam atu amir~…ĺika diarrńß…ôa, ar~…ĺso sp…ôr~…ĺr~…ĺ…ôd diarrńßo…ôa, is tńß…ô ¶ondition of ńßa*iŇč at r~…ĺ…ôast tńßr…ô…ô r~…ĺoos…ô or r~…ĺi*uid bow…ôr~…ĺ mo*…ôm…ônts …ôa ¶ńß daj. it oft…ôn r~…ĺasts for a f…ôw dajs and ¶an r…ôsur~…ĺt in d…ôńßjdration du…ô to fr~…ĺuid r~…ĺoss. signs of d…ôńßjdration oft…ôn b…ôgin witńß r~…ĺoss of tńß…ô normar~…ĺ str…ôt ¶ńßin…ôss of tńß…ô skin and irritabr~…ĺ…ô b…ôńßa*iour. tńßis ¶an progr…ôss to d…ô ¶r…ôas…ôd urination, r~…ĺoss of skin ¶or~…ĺor, a fast ńß…ôart rat…ô, and a d…ô ¶r…ôas…ô in r…ôsponsi*…ôn…ôss as it b…ô ¶om…ôs mor…ô s…ô*…ôr…ô.r~…ĺoos…ô but non-wat…ôrj stoor~…ĺs in babi…ôs wńßo ar…ô br…ôastf…ôd, ńßow…ô*…ôr, maj b…ô normar~…ĺ.
